creator: p5B2EA84B3@t-online.de
text:
Having read this:
https://people.freebsd.org/~linimon/presentations/prs2008/foil02.html

I'm asking myself if I get/got something wrong here.

As I have closed not a few PRs of mine with "Overcome by events" and leaving the note "I do not care anymore either" for not having not received any feedback. This is NOT the case here and I thank you for your input. 

We can assure yourself always and anytime when needed that we spend your remaining lifetime on a volunteer project not getting paid. But what is the message? Shrug, we can not do better. Cope yourself with your ambitiousness.

Is that just the nature of our volunteer project? Well, maybe we do not deserve better ... ?

Don't get me wrong. This is not a personal thing at all. This PR is probably the most unimportant on bugzilla. It's not about THIS PR, just forget about it.

It's about reading "Assignee: ... (nobody)" 

If there were someone instead of nobody, perhaps(?) the PR status might get changed to "open".

I'd like to go into statistics on PRs and maintainer fluctuation for visualizing facts. Any hints on that, BTW?
